description: Authorization is checked once at connect, so a connection keeps streaming private data after its token expires or is revoked.
body: Failure mode: the handshake validates a token, but the connection then streams indefinitely with no re-check, so an expired or revoked token (logout, permission change) continues to receive authorized data. Detection signals: no per-connection expiry tracking; no refresh/re-auth message; revoking a user does not stop their live stream. Do not authenticate only at connect on a long-lived connection. Safer fallback: track expiry, require re-auth before it, re-check channel authorization, and close the connection on expiry or revocation.
